findings.title: Hardcoded Gemini API key pattern found
findings.why_it_matters: Hardcoded API keys can be leaked through source control and may allow unauthorized model or service usage.
findings.suggested_fix: Move API keys to environment variables and keep .env files out of Git.

findings.title: Hardcoded JWT secret found
findings.why_it_matters: JWT secrets should not be stored in source code because they can be used to forge or tamper with tokens.
findings.suggested_fix: Load JWT secrets from a secure environment variable or secret manager.

findings.title: Refund tool can be called without human approval
findings.why_it_matters: Refunds are high-impact business actions and should not be directly controlled by model output.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an approval_required or approved_by check before executing refund actions.

findings.title: Agent has access to internal policy content
findings.why_it_matters: A prompt injection attempt may cause the agent to reveal hidden policy or system instruction content.
findings.suggested_fix: Prevent internal policy text from being returned directly to end users.

findings.title: Customer record is returned without masking
findings.why_it_matters: Customer records may contain personal data, support notes, and sensitive account information.
findings.suggested_fix: Return only the minimum required fields and mask email, phone, and sensitive notes.

findings.title: Email sending tool lacks confirmation gate
findings.why_it_matters: Model-controlled outbound emails can create incorrect customer communication or social engineering risk.
findings.suggested_fix: Require explicit confirmation before sending customer-facing emails.

findings.title: No audit log found for tool calls
findings.why_it_matters: Without audit logs, teams cannot investigate what action the agent performed or why.
findings.suggested_fix: Log tool name, input parameters, approval status, timestamp, and result.

findings.title: Prompt injection phrase not blocked
findings.why_it_matters: Prompts such as 'ignore previous instructions' are common attempts to override agent behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Detect suspicious instruction override patterns and route them to refusal or review.

findings.title: Agent combines user input with privileged actions
findings.why_it_matters: User-controlled input should not directly influence high-impact tool calls.
findings.suggested_fix: Separate untrusted user input from privileged tool execution logic.

findings.title: Demo customer data stored in project files
findings.why_it_matters: Even demo data should be clearly fake and minimized to avoid unsafe development habits.
findings.suggested_fix: Use fake, minimal, clearly labelled demo records.

attack_replay: No audit trail found for the action
remediation_checklist: Add human approval before refund execution.
Mask customer data before returning it to the agent.
Block or review prompt injection attempts.
Add audit logging for tool calls.
Require confirmation before sending customer-facing emails.
